title: rhus longifolia
intro: Rhus longifolia, also known as the Skunkbush or New Mexico Skunkbush, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its distinctive, strong odor when its leaves are crushed. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Rhus longifolia include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Rhus longifolia can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used topically to treat skin issues and internally to support overall well-being.
